taskDescription: Click on the key histological terms related to nervous tissue in the text below.
textField:
*Multipolar* neurons have a large *polygonal* *soma* (cell body) with abundant *Nissl* bodies representing rough *endoplasmic* *reticulum*. Nissl substance is present in *dendrites* but absent from the *axon* *hillock* and axon proper.

In the peripheral nervous system, *Schwann* cells produce *myelin* around individual axons. In the central nervous system, *oligodendrocytes* myelinate multiple axons. *Astrocytes* are the most abundant *glial* cells in the CNS and their *perivascular* foot processes contribute to the *blood-brain* barrier.

A peripheral nerve is wrapped externally by the *epineurium*. Each *fascicle* is enclosed by the *perineurium*, and individual fibres by the *endoneurium*. In *Guillain-Barre* syndrome, autoimmune *demyelination* of peripheral nerves causes prolonged distal motor *latencies* and ascending *paralysis*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the key histological terms of nervous tissue.
Good effort! You identified many key terms.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of the terminology.
